label: fixed-function dataflow NPU
note: quantized-conv dataflow graph: convolutions run at line rate, but the PatchCore distance/argmin/top-k tail is not expressible and spills to the host CPU; general attention is not in the op-set at all. This is the substrate the deploy thesis is about — it forces a bank-free conv detector.
op_support_provenance: coral_edgetpu: COMPILE-VERIFIED (edgetpu_compiler v16.0 partitions the representative graphs — conv fully Mapped to Edge TPU; ArgMin 'unsupported data type' and TopK 'not supported' fall to CPU; see deploy/compile_ops.json / bead j5s.5). hailo: CITED (dataflow compiler is Developer-Zone-gated).

instances.name: coral_edgetpu
instances.label: Coral Edge TPU
instances.sources: S1 (4 TOPS int8), S2 (8 MiB SRAM scratchpad)
instances.note: full-int8 mandatory; 4 TOPS int8, ~8 MiB on-chip scratchpad, model streamed from host. Single-partition CPU fallback amputates the graph at the first unsupported op.

instances.name: hailo_8
instances.label: Hailo-8
instances.sources: S3 (26 TOPS int8, DRAM-free); on-die SRAM capacity not published [S4]
instances.note: 26 TOPS int8; build-time truncation at last CONV2D, pre/post on host. On-die SRAM size is not disclosed in public vendor docs (portal-gated), so the envelope is unknown, not guessed — no off-chip fallback means an over-envelope working set does not fit.

instances.name: hailo_8l
instances.label: Hailo-8L
instances.sources: S7 (13 TOPS int8, DRAM-free); on-die SRAM capacity not published [S5][S6]
instances.note: entry Hailo; 13 TOPS int8, same dataflow + on-die-only constraint as Hailo-8 (SRAM size likewise unpublished).
